name: DSLR Photography
positive: Photo captured with a DSLR camera, shallow depth of field, large sensor clarity, natural optical blur, crisp edge definition, controlled exposure, and balanced tonal range across the frame.

name: Mirrorless Camera
positive: Photo taken using a mirrorless camera, high dynamic range, clean sensor rendering, precise autofocus sharpness, compact optical character, smooth background separation, and accurate color reproduction.

name: Analog Film Photo
positive: Photo shot on analog film, visible grain structure, gentle contrast rolloff, soft highlight bloom, organic tonal transitions, slight exposure imperfections, and chemically derived image texture.

name: 35mm Film Look
positive: Photo captured on 35mm film, pronounced grain, moderate contrast, subtle vignetting, optical softness at edges, natural color shifts, and physical film texture across the image.

name: Medium Format Film
positive: Photo taken with medium format film, extremely fine grain, broad tonal depth, smooth gradients, high detail retention, minimal distortion, and a physically large negative appearance.

name: Large Format Camera
positive: Photo produced using a large format camera, exceptional detail resolution, precise perspective control, extended tonal range, minimal distortion, and extremely shallow optical imperfections.

name: iPhone Camera
positive: Photo captured with an iPhone camera, compact sensor characteristics, computational sharpening, balanced exposure, clean noise reduction, wide lens perspective, and evenly lit digital clarity.

name: Android Smartphone Photo
positive: Photo taken on an Android smartphone, high contrast processing, strong HDR effect, digitally enhanced sharpness, wide-angle lens distortion, and evenly exposed modern mobile aesthetics.

name: Point-and-Shoot Camera
positive: Photo captured with a point-and-shoot camera, deep depth of field, small sensor grain, uniform focus, direct flash balance, and straightforward consumer digital rendering.

name: Studio Flash Photo
positive: Photo lit with studio flash, hard-edged shadows, even exposure, controlled highlights, high subject clarity, minimal motion blur, and precise artificial lighting distribution.

name: On-Camera Flash
positive: Photo taken using on-camera flash, flattened lighting, sharp foreground illumination, strong shadow falloff, reflective highlights, and direct light centered on the subject.

name: Natural Window Light
positive: Photo captured using natural window light, soft directional illumination, gradual shadow transitions, realistic contrast, ambient brightness variation, and gently diffused highlights.

name: Golden Hour Photo
positive: Photo shot during golden hour, warm directional sunlight, elongated shadows, soft contrast, glowing highlights, atmospheric haze, and naturally saturated color tones.

name: Low-Light Photography
positive: Photo captured in low-light conditions, visible sensor noise, high ISO grain, deep shadows, selective illumination, reduced contrast, and localized highlight emphasis.

name: Night Photography
positive: Photo taken at night, dark dominant tones, isolated light sources, high contrast regions, controlled exposure highlights, deep shadow areas, and extended dynamic range handling.

name: Long Exposure Photo
positive: Photo created with long exposure, motion-blurred elements, smooth light trails, stabilized static forms, even tonal blending, and extended time-based visual accumulation.

name: High-Speed Photography
positive: Photo captured with high-speed settings, frozen motion detail, crisp edges, minimal blur, precise timing, controlled lighting, and sharply defined transient forms.

name: Macro Photography
positive: Photo taken with macro setup, extreme close focus, shallow depth of field, enlarged surface detail, optical falloff, precise focal plane, and highly magnified textures.

name: Wide-Angle Lens
positive: Photo captured using a wide-angle lens, expanded field of view, perspective exaggeration, edge stretching, deep focus range, and strong spatial distortion cues.

name: Telephoto Lens
positive: Photo taken with a telephoto lens, compressed perspective, narrow field of view, strong background blur, subject isolation, flattened depth cues, and distant focal clarity.

name: Fisheye Lens
positive: Photo captured using a fisheye lens, extreme wide-angle distortion, circular image framing, pronounced barrel distortion, exaggerated perspective, and a unique spherical visual effect.
